title: Safe MCP Tooling Capability
summary: A capability-pack contract for MCP/tool integrations with manifest, setup, policy, approval defaults, validators, and trace receipts.

retrieval.description: Use when the product needs external tool execution, MCP servers, agent connectors, provider integrations, or governed write-capable tools.

donorSources.label: MCP Registry
donorSources.notes: Metadata-only discovery registry and versioning pattern.

donorSources.label: OpenAI Agents SDK
donorSources.notes: Tool use, multi-agent state, traces, and sandboxed execution patterns.

donorSources.label: OpenAI trace grading
donorSources.notes: Trace-based regression and eval pattern for agent decisions and tool calls.

donorSources.label: GitHub MCP server
donorSources.notes: Official MCP server with configurable toolsets.

donorSources.label: Stripe MCP
donorSources.notes: Public-preview payment tooling with OAuth and confirmation guidance.

prompt: Model external capabilities as versioned tool packs, not as free-form prompt instructions.
Separate discovery metadata from runnable artifacts and policy enforcement.
Read-only tools may be auto-approved after validation; mutating tools must default to explicit approval.
Every tool pack must state what data it can read, what it can write, auth requirements, production risk, and trace requirements.
requiredElements: Machine-readable manifest with provider, version, transport, auth, scopes, and toolsets
Policy for allowed actions, denied actions, escalation, and data handling
Tool-call simulation or trace eval for risky workflows
forbiddenPatterns: write-capable provider tool without approval policy
private credentials embedded in pack files
registry metadata treated as executable authority
remote MCP used without trust or scope notes
allowedSubstitutions: Use read-only docs packs when execution authority is not required.
Use local mock adapters for generated workspaces while preserving real auth/scope shape.
Use internal/private registry metadata when public MCP registry cannot represent private servers.
stackHints: Keep capability packs immutable by version and use a latest alias only as a pointer.
Store trace receipts beside session/runtime receipts, not inside authored pack files.
Split browser automation into read/navigation and write/submit toolsets.

validators.description: Every capability pack must declare transport, auth mode, scopes, and allowed toolsets.
validators.evidence: Capability manifest and setup verification output.

validators.description: Mutating tools must require approval unless a safe automation policy explicitly waives it.
validators.evidence: Policy file, tool metadata, and trace fixture.

validators.description: High-risk tool workflows should carry golden traces or trace-grading fixtures.
validators.evidence: Trace eval fixture and regression result.

provenance.notes: Seeded from MCP and agent-tool ecosystem sidecar research.
